original: Noblewoman: How [i]dare[/i] you insinuate such a thing!
CDress: I-[p/][s/]I wasn't [i]trying[/i] to insult you![p/][s/] I just—!
Noblewoman: Enough![p/] If you try to speak with me again,[p/] I'll have you arrested for harassment!
The noblewoman stormed off in a huff.[p/] Celeste just let out an exhausted sigh.
Every conversation she'd tried hadn't gone any differently.
CDress: ...This isn't working.
ADress: ...I don't think asking them like that will be very effective.
CDress: What?
ADress: Did you expect anyone here to be truthful with you?
ADress: You're essentially asking them if they engaged in adultery.
CDress: Oh...[p/] Hey, Aria.
ADress: I'm surprised to see you here.
ADress: ...You're not "party crashing", are you?
CDress: Of course not![p/][s/] I came here with Ruby and Tyrion!
ADress: ...That honestly just raises more questions,[p/] but I'll take your word for it.
CDress: ...
ADress: I don't know if it's any of my business,[p/] but you should think about whether this is what you truly want.
CDress: What...?
ADress: I understand wanting to find out more about yourself,[p/] but getting entangled in the aristocracy will only bring you misery.
ADress: ...I should know.
CDress: I just want to know more about where I came from.[p/] It might help me understand all this...[p/] weirdness inside me.
ADress: ...I don't think you're weird at all.[p/] I quite enjoy your company.
CDress: Aw...[p/] Really?
ADress: I'm sure Mr. Cuthbert feels the same way.
CDress: ...Really?
ADress: ...Is something wrong?
CDress: N-[p/][s/]No...[p/] It's just that—
CDress: ...Actually,[p/] what do [i]you[/i] think about Tyrion, Aria?
ADress: Well, he's a remarkable attorney.[p/] His deductive skills are incredible.
ADress: Frankly,[p/] I'm a little envious of his talent.[p/] I'm loath to admit that Ruby Tymora has taught him well.
CDress: I meant as a [i]person[/i], Aria.
ADress: ...I only know him in a professional capacity.
CDress: But don't you notice how much he cares about people?
ADress: I suppose he is quite dedicated to his job.
CDress: But it's not just a job to him.[p/] He [i]really[/i] cares about his clients and helping them.
CDress: It doesn't matter how much danger he puts himself in.[p/] It doesn't matter who threatens him.
CDress: He just keeps going,[p/] and it's honestly kind of annoying.
CDress: He won't back down from [i]anyone[/i],[p/] even the Four Pillars.
CDress: Ever since he indicted Pierce,[p/] [i]someone[/i] has been sending people into Tyrion's home.
CDress: I'm good at my job,[p/] but I'm worried I'm going to slip up one day and let him get seriously hurt.
ADress: I am sorry about that.[p/] I wish I could do more to help you with that.
CDress: It's fine.[p/] It's my responsibility to protect him.
ADress: Have you ever told him how worried you've been?
CDress: ...I don't want to stop him from doing what he's doing,[p/] and I don't think he'd even listen if I did.
CDress: That's what makes him who he is.
CDress: He's helping a lot of people.[p/] He inspires them, too.
CDress: He's amazing,[p/] and courageous,[p/] and caring,[p/] and sweet,[p/] and—
ADress: ...Celeste?
CDress: N-[p/][s/]No... I—
CDress: ...I don't know.[p/] Things have been really weird since Aurinax.
ADress: I've been meaning to ask about that.[p/] Did something happen during Dracogen's trial?
CDress: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/] Uh...
CDress: ...We kind of slept together.
ADress: Before marriage?!
CDress: N-[p/][s/]No![p/][s/] Not like [i]that[/i]!
CDress: We just slept in the same bed...[p/] and held hands.
ADress: Before [i]marriage[/i]?!
CDress: I—[p/][p/] ...What?
ADress: I-[p/][s/]I suppose everyone has different levels of...[p/] libido,[p/] but you shouldn't be so reckless.
CDress: ...Aria,[p/] I'm not going to get pregnant from holding Tyrion's hand.
ADress: Even so,[p/] I'm not sure what kind of woman you think I am.
ADress: I do not wish to indulge you in your lecherous tales of illicit hand-holding.
CDress: Oh, like [i]you've[/i] never wanted to hold Aster's hand or anything like that?
ADress: I would [i]never[/i]![p/][s/] I only have the utmost respect for him!
CDress: Come on![p/][s/] I was [i]there[/i] at the Refinery![p/][s/] It's obvious you like him like that.
ADress: Please.[p/] You're grossly exaggerating things.
CDress: ...And what if I told you he was standing right behind you?
ADress: Do you honestly think I'd fall for such an obvious lie?
Aster: What are you falling for?
ADress: A-[p/][s/]Ah?!
Aria's entire body whipped around to face her fiancé.[p/] She almost tripped as she stumbled backwards.
Her voice started to break into a soft, high-pitched whimper.
ADress: Y-[p/][s/]Y-[p/][s/]Y-[p/][s/]Your highness?!
Celeste noticed how Aria's eyes were frequently darting between the Crown Prince's face and his hand.
Alaric: Oh.[p/] Hello, Lady Steelwind.
Knight: Your Highness![p/] Please don't run ahead like that.
After my otherworldly encounter,[p/] I decided to head back to the ballroom and wait for Celeste.
I was able to find a seat somewhere,[p/] and it gave me a chance to think over what had happened.
I'd solved some sort of riddle—[p/]it mentioned illumination and shadows.
Horus was also the God of Light,[p/] and those earrings were of Luminian origin.
Did my Eye of Horus react to them?[p/] Lord Haroldson had probably owned those earrings for years,[p/] yet he didn't see anything.
And then there was this—[p/] Actually,[p/] I had no idea [i]what[/i] this "Divine Edict" was.
Was it a prayer?[p/] A riddle?[p/] An ability?
"A divine invocation to manifest any covenant written in blood and spirit"...
What did that even mean?[p/] It just sounded like some flowery nonsense the Church of Bahamut would say.
If it was some kind of spell,[p/] it was nothing like the Arcane spells I'd seen in Compendiums.
The descriptions of [i]those[/i] spells were far more straightforward.
...Ugh.[p/] It just felt like the more I learned about myself,[p/] the more questions I found.
...Scaled Lord,[p/] how long had I been staring at that girl?
My eyes just locked onto the first thing across the room,[p/] but it was clear she noticed me looking at her.
I quickly looked away,[p/] but as I glanced back,[p/] she tried to flash me a friendly smile.
She didn't look offended,[p/] which was a relief.[p/]
I'd imagine any other noblewoman would use this as an excuse to have me thrown out.
...Actually,[p/] she seemed to be the only person in the room who wasn't disgusted by my presence.
Maybe Celeste was right.[p/]
I needed to learn to approach new people,[p/] and fate was giving me an opportunity.
As I walked across the room,[p/] several nobles gave me the side-eye.
She was surrounded by several attendants,[p/] who moved to stop me from approaching her.
However,[p/] she waved her hand to dismiss them.[p/] That was a good sign.
Her attendants seemed to disapprove,[p/] but they didn't raise any objections and left quietly.
On a closer look,[p/] she seemed to be a noble of some renown,[p/] but she seemed to exhibit a modesty that most aristocrats lacked.
She was actually quite beautiful,[p/] not as beautiful as Celeste,[p/] but—
...Ugh,[p/] what was I thinking?
I shouldn't be objectifying them like that.
I needed to keep my thoughts clearer.[p/] I couldn't just—
I realized I was just standing in front of her,[p/] silently having this conversation with myself.
She looked confused.[p/] I needed to say something.[p/] Uh...[p/] How [i]did[/i] normal people talk?
Should I say a formal greeting,[p/] or would that just come off as pretentious?
Maybe I should say something more casual,[p/] like Celeste would.
I could make some kind of observation to connect with her somehow.
Agh![p/][s/] Come on, Tyrion![p/][s/] Just say something!
Tyrion: Hey, my fellow blonde.[p/][s/] What's—[p/] What's going on?
...This memory would haunt me for the rest of my life.
Laefe: Um...[p/] Hello.
I cleared my throat.
Tyrion: ...I-[p/][s/]I'm sorry if I was staring...[p/] I was just...[p/] daydreaming, I guess.
Laefe: Oh,[p/] that's okay.
Tyrion: I wasn't staring at you.
Tyrion: Er—[p/][s/] That's not to say,[p/][s/] that I [i]wouldn't[/i] intentionally stare at you!
Laefe: Um...
Tyrion, what the hell are you saying?![p/][s/] She's going to think you're a psychopath!
Recalibrate![p/][s/] Change the subject!
Social interactions occurred for the sake of engaging individuals in activities!
What activity would someone do at the Lords' Ball?!
Tyrion: ...Would you like to dance?
Tyrion![p/][s/] You don't know how to dance!
Laefe: ...Okay.
CDress: Hey, Aster.[p/] What do you think of Aria's dress?
Aster: It suits you quite well, Aria.[p/] You look lovely.
ADress: [i]*squeak*[/i]
Alaric: ...Gross.
Aster: But I must say, Ms. McCoy.[p/] I'm surprised to see you here.
Aster: How did you get an invite to the Lords' Ball?
CDress: I'm just tagging along for one of Ruby's jobs.
Aster: Ah,[p/] that explains why Mr. Cuthbert is here as well.
CDress: Oh,[p/] Tyrion's back?[p/] Where is he?
Her eyes scanned the ballroom until she found Tyrion...
CDress: ...Who the hell is that?
Aster: Oh,[p/] I believe that's Lady Frega.
Aster: That's strange.[p/] She usually rebukes most men who approach her.
CDress: Oh, she does, does she?
CDress: I guess Tyrion's special for some reason.
CDress: ...He never told me he could dance.
They were stumbling along the dance floor.
The Crown Prince watched as Tyrion stepped on Lady Frega's toes several times.
Aster: ...I don't think he actually can.
CDress: What would a Frega want with Tyrion?
CDress: She's up to something...
CDress: Aria,[p/] what do you know about her?
ADress: Hraaaaaaaaah...
Aria was still staring blankly at the Crown Prince.
It looked like she was running through several calculations in her head,[p/] but failing to arrive at anything.
CDress: Ugh...
Another misstep caused me to step on her foot.
Tyrion: I am [i]so[/i] sorry.[p/] I don't do this a lot.
...Or at all.
Laefe: I-[p/][s/]It's okay![p/][s/] At least,[p/] you're—
She winced in pain as I accidentally kicked her foot again.
It felt like I was slowly chipping away at that considerate smile on her face.
Laefe: Perhaps you should follow [i]my[/i] lead.
I carefully looked downwards,[p/] watching to make sure I didn't accidentally parry her foot with mine.
It honestly made it easier.[p/] Looking at her face presented its own distraction.
Tyrion: ...I'm Tyrion, by the way.
Laefe: Yes,[p/] I'm aware.[p/] You've been in the paper a lot recently.
Tyrion: Oh...
Laefe: I'm Laefe...[p/] of House Frega.
Tyrion: ...Oh!
Of all the people I could have approached,[p/] I chose someone from one of the Four Pillars Families...
...But she didn't seem too bothered.[p/] Why would she agree to dance with me if she knew who I was?
Laefe: ...I'm sorry for not bringing it up sooner.
Laefe: I can imagine you wouldn't want to associate with someone like me.
Tyrion: N-[p/][s/]No![p/][s/] It's just that—
Tyrion: I had a run-in with your sister a few months ago.
Tyrion: I don't think she cares for me that much.
Laefe: ...Then it sounds like we have something in common.
Laefe: I—
Before she could finish that thought,[p/] she suddenly froze.
I almost tripped as I avoided stepping on her foot again.
She was staring wide-eyed at something behind me.
Her breathing was quickening,[p/] and her hand began to squeeze my shoulder.
I turned to look and saw Beatrice storming through the ballroom.
Laefe: Is that—?!
Laefe: I-[p/][s/]I'm terribly sorry![p/][s/] I have to go!
Without another word,[p/] she quickly left,[p/] hurrying after Beatrice.
Did the two of them know each other?
CDress: ...Ah,[p/] did you get snubbed?
Tyrion: Oh, thank the Scaled Lord—[p/]someone I actually know.
CDress: ...So?[p/][s/] Who was that?
She wore her usual approachable smile,[p/] but I could've sworn something about her tone was louder than usual.
Tyrion: I was...[p/] trying to talk to people.[p/] Isn't that what you told me to do?
CDress: Yeah,[p/] but I didn't mean that you should shoot your shot with a Frega.
CDress: You're going to tell me how you pulled [i]that[/i] off.
Tyrion: ...Do I have to?
CDress: Yes.
CDress: Sit down.[p/] I snuck you some bread rolls from the buffet.
Tyrion: Oh,[p/][s/] have you tried them?![p/][s/] They're amazing!
CDress: Yeah,[p/] yeah...
Aster: It really [i]is[/i] you!
Aster: Where have you been all this time, Beatrice?![p/][s/] We've been so worried about you!
Beatrice: ...Really?
ADress: I-[p/][s/]It appears you two have much to talk about.[p/] I'll be taking my leave.
Beatrice: ...
Beatrice: I've known for a while,[p/] but I still can't believe it...
Beatrice: You're [i]actually[/i] marrying her, aren't you?
Aster: ...You didn't leave me much choice.
Aster: As the future king of Wyverngarde,[p/] I have responsibilities.
Aster: ...I doubt [i]you[/i] would know anything about that.
Beatrice: Hmph...[p/] When did [i]you[/i] suddenly start caring so much about "responsibility"?
Aster: I've always done what I had to for the people of Wyverngarde.
Beatrice: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] ...I barely even recognize you anymore.
Aster: I'd certainly hope so,[p/] considering what I was like back then.
Beatrice: I've always done what was necessary to stay in control,[p/] but what you're doing to Aria...[p/] It's—
Aster: I know,[p/] but she's willing to put aside her disdain of me for the sake of Wyverngarde.
Beatrice: Nine hells,[p/] do you seriously still not—?
Beatrice: ...Never mind.[p/] It seems that you're just as much of an oaf as you were before.
Aster: And your words still have such a unique way of cutting into me like a knife.
Aster: I oddly missed that.
Beatrice just scoffed at that.
CDress: Oh my god,[p/] please say it again.
Tyrion: ...I really don't want to.
CDress: Come on![p/][s/] The look on your face gets better each time.
Tyrion: [i]*sigh*[/i]
Tyrion: ...Hey, my fellow blonde.[p/] What's going on?
Celeste just started snickering to herself again.
She started slamming her fist on the table,[p/] drawing some looks from the aristocrats nearby.
CDress: I can't believe it.
CDress: I guess any line will work when it comes from you.
Tyrion: I'm just glad it's over...[p/] I looked like an idiot out there.
CDress: Hey,[p/] it's not over yet.[p/] You owe me a dance.
Tyrion: ...What?
CDress: Don't be like that.[p/] How often do we get to go to a party like this?
Tyrion: I—
Before I had a chance to object,[p/] she grabbed my hand and started dragging me to the middle of the dance floor.
Tyrion: C-[p/][s/]Celeste![p/][s/] People are staring at us...
CDress: Who cares?[p/] They're nobles.
CDress: They're going to hate us no matter what we do.
She took hold of my other hand and pulled me into a waltz.
Tyrion: Celeste,[p/] I have no idea what I'm doing.
CDress: It's easy.[p/] Just stop being so awkward about it.
That was easy for her to say...
Tyrion: Alright,[p/] can you at least tell me what to do?
CDress: Well, first...
CDress: You need to be closer to me.
With one swift motion,[p/] she pulled my body closer to hers.
Tyrion: ...!
Tyrion: I-[p/][s/]I don't remember being this close when I was dancing with Laefe.
CDress: Yeah?[p/] Well,[p/] I'm not Laefe.
CDress: Watch where I'm going and follow my lead.
Tyrion: Y-[p/][s/]You seem to know a lot about these things.
CDress: I had to do a lot of networking when I was a mercenary.
CDress: Do you know how many times I had to charm a merchant into hiring me?
Tyrion: I-[p/][s/]I see.
I carefully watched the movements of her feet and tried to match them.
After a while,[p/] we both fell into a gentle rhythm.
I accidentally stepped on her foot,[p/] but she didn't seem to react at all.
Was she being polite,[p/] or did she genuinely not feel any pain?
CDress: ...You're getting it,[p/] but don't focus so much on where your feet are.
CDress: Just look at me.
Tyrion: W-[p/][s/]What?
CDress: Come on, look up.
I finally looked up at her,[p/] and she smiled back at me.
It was embarrassing being this close,[p/] but every time I avoided her eyes,[p/] I found myself being drawn back into them.
CDress: Ah, [i]there[/i] he is![p/] The [i]infamous[/i] Tyrion Cuthbert.
CDress: He never turns away from challenging a noble,[p/] but he can't dance a step to save his life.
I was starting to become lightheaded for some reason.
Was I sweating too much?[p/][s/] I really hoped it wasn't visible.
Every part of my body was tense,[p/] but I didn't want to break away from her.
There was a comforting warmth behind all this.[p/] Things were always different with her.
I let myself relax a bit more and continued matching her movements.
CDress: See?[p/] There you go.
CDress: But...
Tyrion: ...What is it?
CDress: You know the man is supposed to lead, right?
Tyrion: ...Maybe I don't subscribe to gender stereotypes.
CDress: [i]Or[/i] maybe you just suck at this.
...I'd forgotten what it was like to let go like this.
It always felt like there was something to prepare for—[p/]a client that needed saving,[p/] a killer that needed catching.
I'd been so caught up in trying to learn more about the Eye of Horus that I was ignoring other thoughts I'd been having.
Tyrion: ...
I took a deep breath.
Tyrion: Can we talk about what happened in Aurinax...?
CDress: Oh...
I felt her arm tense around my shoulder.
Tyrion: ...It was weird, wasn't it?[p/] Us sleeping together?
Tyrion: Or—[p/][s/]I mean—[p/][s/] I know we didn't "sleep" together,[p/][s/] but—
I was nailing this.[p/] I was absolutely nailing this.
CDress: N-[p/][s/]No...[p/] I get it...[p/] It wasn't exactly professional.
Tyrion: Yes![p/][s/] [i]Exactly[/i]![p/][s/] It was very unprofessional of me.
Tyrion: Even if it was an accident,[p/] I'm worried that I overstepped my boundaries as your employer.
CDress: Well,[p/] [i]Ruby's[/i] my employer.[p/] You're different.
Tyrion: Different...?[p/] How?
CDress: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/][s/] Uh...
She seemed to think about it for a moment before she sighed to herself.
CDress: ...I guess you're the only person who doesn't treat me like a monster.
Tyrion: ...Because you're a mage?
CDress: Come on, Tyrion...[p/] You've seen me fight.
CDress: I know you're just being polite about it,[p/] but it's off-putting, right?
Tyrion: It really isn't.[p/] What are you talking about?
CDress: Really?[p/] It doesn't freak you out whenever I beat up a crowd of thugs in an alleyway?
CDress: Or how about last night when I ragdolled that thief trying to break into your house?
Tyrion: ...I was honestly more impressed by that than anything else.
CDress: Stop being nice about it!
CDress: I [i]know[/i] it's not normal.
CDress: ...Something inside me really enjoys fighting.[p/] I love it,[p/] but I also hate it.
CDress: Every time someone's seen me fight,[p/] they start looking at me like I'm some kind of bloodthirsty freak.
CDress: Even my parents used to look at me like that sometimes.
Tyrion: ...I'm really sorry if [i]I've[/i] ever looked at you like that.
CDress: That's the worst part![p/] You haven't.
CDress: I never know what you're really thinking.
CDress: You always charge headfirst into danger without a second thought.
CDress: This whole kingdom is rotting with corruption.[p/] I don't get how you can be so hopeful and optimistic all the time.
CDress: I really admire you for that,[p/] but sometimes it feels too good to be true.
CDress: It makes me wonder what's really going on inside your head.
Tyrion: You're really passionate about protecting the people you care about.
Tyrion: When you fight,[p/] I see someone who cares deeply.
Tyrion: You love the people around you,[p/] so much so that you're driven to stop anyone who threatens them.
Tyrion: You make it all look so effortless.[p/] I don't think I've ever met anyone as strong as you.
Tyrion: I thought you were always proud of that.[p/] It's one of the things I've always admired about you.
I saw a smile curl on her lips as her face became flushed.
Tyrion: You know the truth about me.[p/] I'm not some idealistic hero.
Tyrion: I'm just a dumb traumatized kid who thinks he can save everyone.
We both stayed silent for a while,[p/] simply moving to the beat of the music.
CDress: ...Tyrion,[p/] I didn't mind being unprofessional with you.
Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/][s/] That's good.
CDress: But only if you were okay with that.
CDress: ...[i]Would[/i] you be okay with things like that?
Tyrion: Oh...[p/] Uh—
I still wasn't certain about what she meant,[p/][p/] or why she suddenly switched to the present tense.
Was she trying to imply that she also wanted to—?
...No,[p/] I couldn't rush forward before I had more evidence.
I needed to be tactful...[p/] and vague.
Tyrion: Well,[p/][s/] I appreciate some level of professionalism in my work,[p/][s/] but with you—
Tyrion: Er—[p/][s/] I mean—
Tyrion: Or that is to say,[p/][s/] that I'd like to attempt—
Tyrion: Agh![p/][s/] That's not to say that I don't not want to only be professional with you!
CDress: ...What?
Scaled Lord,[p/][s/] I just needed to say what I wanted to say!
Tyrion: ...I've never felt this way about someone before.
Tyrion: I don't know how to navigate this,[p/] but I want to—[p/] If you're okay with it—
Tyrion: Um...[p/][s/] Could we try—?
I suddenly heard the footfalls of several knights behind me.
Celeste's eyes widened.[p/] Her lips pursed.
Confused,[p/] I began to turn my head to see what the commotion was,[p/] but she just grabbed me by the chin.
CDress: Hey,[p/] why don't you finish that thought first?
Orym: Beatrice Frega!
Did he just say "Beatrice [i]Frega[/i]"?
Tyrion: O-[s/][p/]Oh...[p/][s/] Um...[p/][s/] I think something's happening.
CDress: Nope![p/][s/] It's just Orym!
CDress: Turns out he's just an absolute monster on the dance floor!
Well,[p/] [i]that[/i] just made me want to look more.
Orym: By the authority of the Wyverngarde crown,[p/][s/] you’re under arrest!
CDress: Seriously?![p/][s/] Now?!
Tyrion: ...W-[p/][s/]We should go see what's happening.
CDress: Yeah...
Aster: You are [i]completely[/i] out of line, Commander!
Aster: I thought I told you to stand down!
Alaric: Do you not recognize who you're speaking to?!
Alaric: Let go of Lady Frega!
Orym: U-[p/][s/]Um...[p/] I can't really do that, Your Highnesses.
As we approached Beatrice,[p/] we saw the two princes standing between the Inquisition and Beatrice.
Tyrion: Your Highness,[p/] what's going on?
The Crown Prince scanned the room.[p/] Everyone's eyes were on him.
He leaned towards me and spoke in a hushed whisper.
Aster: It's William Frega.[p/] He was found murdered in his study.
Aster: And these cretins think that [i]Beatrice[/i] is behind it somehow.
Orym: S-[p/][s/]Sorry, Your Highness,[p/] but my orders come straight from Steelwind.
Alaric: Lady Steelwind sent you?
Beatrice cursed under her breath and clapped her hands together.
Beatrice: I should go with them.
Alaric: What?![p/][s/] No!
Aster: My brother is right![p/] They have no right to—
Beatrice: We're causing a scene,[p/] and I don't want to cause friction between you and Aria.
Beatrice: She's always been meticulous.[p/] I'm sure she's just being careful...
Beatrice: I'm sure I'll be exonerated once they've thoroughly investigated what happened.
Aster: ...Okay.
Alaric: What?![p/][s/] You can't just let them take her away!
Aster: ...Brother.[p/] She's correct.[p/] It is not our place to interfere with the laws of our kingdom.
Alaric: B-[p/][s/]But—
Aster: ...[i]However[/i].
Orym: ...Y-[p/][s/]Yes, Your Highness?
Aster: Ms. Beatrice is blind.[p/] One of your knights will need to escort her.
Orym: ...She's what?!
Orym: Uh...[p/] Anyways,[p/] we're going to need to take you to a holding cell, lady.
Orym: One of you should stay with her and guide her through—
Beatrice: Please.[p/] I'm not a child.
Beatrice walked past the knights and toward the foyer.
It appeared that she already knew her way out.
Orym: ...
Tyrion: ...Shouldn't you all be following her?
Orym: R-[p/][s/]Right![p/][s/] Inquisition,[p/][s/] move out!
It seemed to be just my luck that a night like this would end in a murder...
What the hell was going on?
Player: Chandalier
Tyrion: These chandeliers look fancy enough to be worth a small fortune.
Celeste: The Banking Houses are loaded, aren't they?
Player: Flowers
Tyrion: Each of these tables has a centerpiece of flowers on it.
Celeste: To be honest,[p/] it kind of looks tacky.
Tyrion: I'm sure their notion of aesthetics must fly over the heads of peasants like us...
Player: Back

It almost looked like a room that I'd encounter in the Frega Manor.
As I entered,[p/] I spotted several armed men standing at various points in the room.
...And most surprisingly,[p/] I saw Eris standing near the window.
I shot her a confused look,[p/] but she simply smiled and waved at me.
Sitting at a desk next to her,[p/] was a man with an eyepatch.
He looked friendly and welcoming,[p/] but my Eye of Horus couldn't read anything from him.
It was like trying to read thoughts from a stone.
He felt reminiscent of those charlatans trying to sell me suspicious "miracle" potions on the street.
Eyetaker: Ah, Tymora.
Upon seeing the two of us,[p/] he cordially welcomed my mentor before turning his attention to me.
Eyetaker: And [i]you[/i] must be Tyrion.[p/] Tymora has told me a lot about you.

Eyetaker: Hm...[p/] I've been called many names over the years,[p/] but my favorite has always been "The Eyetaker".

Tyrion: ...Ms. Tymora.[p/] What is this...?

Eris was his [i]daughter[/i]?!
Eyetaker: But I have to say,[p/] I'm quite impressed.
Eyetaker: Getting a [i]monkey[/i] to testify?[p/][s/] I don't think [i]I[/i] could even pull off a grift like that.
Eyetaker: You've pretty much blown past my expectations.[p/] Why I—
Tyrion: Get to the point.
As soon as I interrupted him,[p/] I felt every person in the room tense up.
Eyetaker: ...Relax, everyone.[p/] Kids will be kids.
Eyetaker: I'm a father myself, you know?
Eris: ...
Eyetaker: I'll get straight to the point.
Eyetaker: I have a certain interest in the outcome of this trial.
Eyetaker: Little Lady Frega has been a client of ours for a long time.
Eyetaker: I've done quite a lot of favors for her.[p/] It's been quite an investment, you see?

Eyetaker: But there's no need to worry.[p/] I know she's good for it.
Eyetaker: That is...[p/] [i]if[/i] she can inherit House Frega.

I narrowed my eyes at him,[p/] then to Ms. Tymora.

Eyetaker: [i]He[/i] was the one who came to [i]my[/i] people for help.
Eyetaker: I just told him to pay back what he owed.
Eyetaker: If he hadn't come to me,[p/] it would have been some other criminal locked up with him.
Eyetaker: But that's beside the point.[p/] Enough about what I wanted from Justin.

Tyrion: No matter [i]what[/i] happens,[p/] the courts [i]will[/i] blame someone for this.

Eyetaker: That way,[p/] everyone can come out of this unharmed.

Nobleman: "In the event that Jaiden von Sanctus does [i]not[/i] enter into such an engagement within the specified time frame,[p/] my assets and property shall be distributed as such."
Nobleman: "All of House Frega will be divided equally among my children,[p/] as per the laws of our kingdom."
I was right.[p/] William Frega never disinherited Beatrice.
And since Beatrice [i]would[/i] receive a portion of William's inheritance,[p/] she had a motive to commit the murder.
I glanced at Miriam.[p/] It was hard to read her expression,[p/] but something appeared to be on her mind.
Without a word,[p/] she walked out of the room,[p/] ignoring both Beatrice and Laefe.
...Laefe,[p/] on the other hand,[p/] seemed to be in good spirits.

...Leaving Celeste and me alone with Ms. Tymora.

Ruby: You are [i]going[/i] to withdraw.[p/] This is not up for discussion.

Maybe she was right...[p/] Maybe she knows more about what's going on than I did,[p/] and she was still my mentor.
